title: A song I shared with a friend
summary: Tell the story of a song that connects you to a particular friendship.
topic: A song I shared with a friend
prompt: Write 150–250 words about one song that you connect to a friend. Describe when you first listened to it together and how the song makes you feel. Use past simple and time linkers (when, after).
hintStarters: I first heard this song when…
Every time I hear it now, I…
sampleText: There is one slow song that I always connect to my friend Aisha. We were sixteen, and we were spending our summer holiday at her aunt's house in Istanbul. One evening, we climbed to the rooftop with two cups of tea and one pair of headphones. We shared the headphones, one for each ear. The sun was going down behind the mosques, and the sky was orange and soft pink. Aisha pressed play. The song was slow and quiet, only a guitar and one woman's voice. We did not say anything. We just listened. When the song finished, Aisha asked me to play it again. We listened three more times. The lyrics were simple, but they felt important on that rooftop. After that summer, we did not see each other for two years. She moved to Canada, and I went to university in another city. But every time I hear that song now, I am back on the rooftop with her, sharing one pair of headphones, watching the orange sun. The song is not famous, and it is not even my favorite. But it is the only song that can bring me back to that exact evening. That is enough for me.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: every time I hear that song
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mỗi khi tôi nghe bài hát đó

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past continuous + past simple
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'The sun was going down behind the mosques' (past continuous, mô tả bối cảnh) kết hợp với hành động ngắn ở past simple ('Aisha pressed play').
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'The sun was going down behind the mosques' (past continuous, background) combines with a short action in past simple ('Aisha pressed play').

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Linker 'after'
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: 'After that summer, we did not see each other for two years.' — 'After' chỉ thời điểm tiếp theo trong câu chuyện.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: 'After that summer, we did not see each other for two years.' 'After' marks the next time point in the story.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một bài hát chậm mà tôi luôn gắn với cô bạn Aisha. Chúng tôi mười sáu tuổi, và đang nghỉ hè tại nhà cô của bạn ấy ở Istanbul. Một buổi tối, chúng tôi leo lên mái nhà với hai cốc trà và một đôi tai nghe. Chúng tôi chia đôi tai nghe, mỗi đứa một bên. Mặt trời đang lặn sau các nhà thờ Hồi giáo, bầu trời màu cam và hồng nhạt. Aisha nhấn nút phát. Bài hát chậm và êm, chỉ có một cây ghi-ta và giọng một người phụ nữ. Chúng tôi không nói gì. Chỉ lắng nghe. Khi bài hát kết thúc, Aisha xin tôi bật lại. Chúng tôi đã nghe thêm ba lần nữa. Lời bài hát đơn giản, nhưng trên mái nhà đó chúng có ý nghĩa quan trọng. Sau mùa hè đó, chúng tôi không gặp nhau hai năm liền. Bạn ấy chuyển sang Canada, còn tôi học đại học ở thành phố khác. Nhưng mỗi khi tôi nghe bài hát đó bây giờ, tôi lại trở về trên mái nhà cùng bạn ấy, chia chung đôi tai nghe, nhìn mặt trời màu cam. Bài hát không nổi tiếng, và cũng không phải bài tôi thích nhất. Nhưng đó là bài duy nhất có thể đưa tôi trở lại buổi tối chính xác đó. Với tôi, vậy là đủ.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best opens this memory?
options: There is one slow song that I always connect to my friend Aisha.
There is one slow song that I am always connecting to my friend Aisha.
There is one slow song that I always connected to my friend Aisha.
There a slow song that I always connect to my friend Aisha.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple is natural for a current, ongoing feeling about a memory.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense fits a permanent feeling?

options: The sun is going down behind the mosques.
The sun was going down behind the mosques.
The sun went down behind the mosques while was going.
The sun goes down behind the mosques.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past continuous ('was going down') fits the slow, ongoing background.

options: We shared the headphone, one for each ears.
We share the headphones, one for each ear.
We shared the headphones, one for each ear.
We are sharing the headphones, one for ear each.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Headphones' is always plural; past simple matches the story.

options: After that summer, we do not see each other for two years.
After that summer, we have not seen each other since two years.
After that summer, we are not seeing each other for two years.
After that summer, we did not see each other for two years.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple matches a finished period.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep the tense in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past form of 'press'.
options: Aisha pressed play.
Aisha press play.
Aisha is pressing play.
Aisha has pressed play in that day.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural in English?
options: Every time I am hearing that song now, I back on the rooftop with her.
Every time I hear that song now, I am back on the rooftop with her.
Every time I hear that song now, I have been back on the rooftop with her.
Each time when I hearing that song now, I am back on the rooftop with her.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Every time + present simple, present simple' is the standard pattern.
mcQuestions.hint: Match present simple in both halves.

mcQuestions.prompt: Pick the most natural verb phrase.
options: We are listening three more times.
We have listened three more time.
We listened three more times.
We listening three time more.
mcQuestions.explanation: Past simple of 'listen' is 'listened', and 'three more times' is correct word order.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for correct past form and word order.

options: When the song was finishing, Aisha asks me to play it again.
When the song finish, Aisha asked me to play again it.
When the song has finished, Aisha is asking me to play it again.
When the song finished, Aisha asked me to play it again.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both clauses in past simple keep the timeline clean.
mcQuestions.hint: Keep both clauses in the past.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence shows a clear contrast?
options: The song is not famous, but it is the only song that brings me back.
The song is not famous, because it is the only song that brings me back.
The song is not famous, so it is the only song that brings me back.
The song not famous, but it the only song that brings me back.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'But' shows the contrast; the rest of the grammar is intact.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the most personal, concrete closing.
options: In conclusion, music is wonderful.
That is enough for me.
Music is the universal language of the soul.
It is important to note that songs are powerful.
mcQuestions.explanation: A short, personal closing beats a generic AI-style line.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Is it the song that holds the memory, or the moment? If you forgot the song, would the memory feel the same?
